summary: Open reduction of a condylar fracture is absolutely required when there is significant lateral displacement or dislocation due to its direct impact on joint alignment and long-term functional outcomes. This disruption leads to compromised articular surface congruence, increasing risk of early-onset arthritic changes if not corrected surgically.
